In [140]:
# CROSS TABULATION
# Satisfaction & return rate per platform
platform_sat = df.groupby('Preferred_Platform')['Satisfaction'].mean().round(2)

platform_return = df.groupby('Preferred_Platform')['Returned_Product'] \
    .apply(lambda x: (x == 'Yes').mean() * 100).round(1)

result = pd.DataFrame({'Avg Satisfaction': platform_sat, 'Return Rate %': platform_return})
print(result.sort_values('Avg Satisfaction', ascending=False))

                    Avg Satisfaction  Return Rate %
Preferred_Platform                                 
Ajio                            3.84           24.0
Amazon                          3.80           20.0
Flipkart                        3.39           54.8
Myntra                          3.21           52.6
In [141]:
#Cross-tabulation: age vs platform
age_platform = pd.crosstab(df['Age'], df['Preferred_Platform'])
print(age_platform)

print("\nRow totals (most active age group):")
print(age_platform.sum(axis=1))

Preferred_Platform  Ajio  Amazon  Flipkart  Myntra
Age                                               
18–24                  3       6         3       7
25–34                  4       5         6       4
35–44                  5       5         7       3
45–54                  9       2         7       5
55+                    4       7         8       0

payment_counts = df['Preferred_Payment_Method'].value_counts()

Name: count, dtype: int64
In [143]:
# Most preferred payment method used by customer
most_common = payment_counts.idxmax()
highest_count = payment_counts.max()
print(f"\nThe most preferred payment method is '{most_common}' with {highest_count} users.")

The most preferred payment method is 'Net Banking' with 21 users.
In [146]:
spending_map = {
    "<1000"     : 500,
    "1000–3000" : 2000,
    "3001–5000" : 4000,
    "5001–10000": 7500,
    ">10000"    : 12000,
}

df["Spending_Numeric"] = df["Monthly_Spending"].map(spending_map)

In [164]:
import numpy as np
In [165]:
#The average, highest, and lowest satisfaction scores
satisfaction = df['Satisfaction'].to_numpy()
reviews = df['Reviews_Importance'].to_numpy()
gender = df['Gender'].to_numpy()
In [168]:
# Mean,max and min
avg_sat = np.mean(satisfaction)
max_sat = np.max(satisfaction)
min_sat = np.min(satisfaction)
In [169]:
print(f"Average Customer Satisfaction: {avg_sat:.2f}")
print(f"Highest Satisfaction Score: {max_sat}")
print(f"Lowest Satisfaction Score: {min_sat}\n")

Average Customer Satisfaction: 3.57
Highest Satisfaction Score: 5
Lowest Satisfaction Score: 1
In [170]:
#Create a filter (mask) to separate Male and Female data
male_filter = (gender == 'Male')
female_filter = (gender == 'Female')
In [171]:
avg_male_sat = np.mean(satisfaction[male_filter])
avg_female_sat = np.mean(satisfaction[female_filter])
In [172]:
print(f"Average Satisfaction for Men: {avg_male_sat:.2f}")
print(f"Average Satisfaction for Women: {avg_female_sat:.2f}\n")

Average Satisfaction for Men: 3.51
Average Satisfaction for Women: 3.61
In [201]:
# Fill categorical missing values with mode
categorical_cols = df.select_dtypes(include='object').columns

for col in categorical_cols:
    df[col].fillna(df[col].mode()[0], inplace=True)

print("\nMissing values after cleaning:")
print(df.isnull().sum())

C:\Users\rutuj\AppData\Local\Temp\ipykernel_25748\139833736.py:5: FutureWarning: A value is trying to be set on a copy of a DataFrame or Series through chained assignment using an inplace method.
The behavior will change in pandas 3.0. This inplace method will never work because the intermediate object on which we are setting values always behaves as a copy.

For example, when doing 'df[col].method(value, inplace=True)', try using 'df.method({col: value}, inplace=True)' or df[col] = df[col].method(value) instead, to perform the operation inplace on the original object.

In [205]:
#Using first two categorical columns
if len(categorical_cols) >= 2:
    crosstab = pd.crosstab(
        df[categorical_cols[0]],
        df[categorical_cols[1]],
        margins=True
    )

    print("\nCross Tabulation:")
    display(crosstab)

In [211]:
if len(categorical_cols) > 0:

    plt.figure(figsize=(7, 7))

    df[categorical_cols[0]].value_counts().plot(
        kind='pie',
        autopct='%1.1f%%'
    )

    plt.ylabel("")
    plt.title(f"Percentage Distribution of {categorical_cols[0]}")
    plt.show()
In [148]:
data  = df["Preferred_Platform"].value_counts()

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 4))              # width, height in inches
plt.bar(data.index, data.values,       # x labels, y numbers
        color="#2563eb", width=0.5)
plt.title("Platform Popularity")
plt.xlabel("Platform")
plt.ylabel("Number of people")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [149]:
data = df["City"].value_counts()

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
plt.barh(data.index, data.values,      # barh = horizontal
         color="#7c3aed")
plt.title("Respondents by City")
plt.xlabel("Count")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [153]:
# Monthly income by gender spend 
sns.boxplot(
    x='Gender',
    y='Monthly_Income',
    data=df
)

plt.title("Income Distribution by Gender")
plt.show()
In [158]:
plt.figure(figsize=(7, 5))
sns.boxplot(data=df, x='Returned_Product', y='Satisfaction', width=0.4)
sns.stripplot(data=df, x='Returned_Product', y='Satisfaction', color='black', alpha=0.3, size=6)

plt.title('Customer Satisfaction vs. Product Return Status', fontsize=14, pad=15, weight='bold')
plt.xlabel('Has the Customer Returned a Product?', fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel('Satisfaction Score', fontsize=12)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [159]:
plt.figure(figsize=(7, 4.5))
sns.countplot(data=df, x='Satisfaction')

plt.title('Distribution of Customer Satisfaction Ratings', fontsize=14, pad=15, weight='bold')
plt.xlabel('Satisfaction Score (1 = Lowest, 5 = Highest)', fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel('Number of Customers', fontsize=12)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [177]:
# Comparing age group and platform together
age_platform = pd.crosstab(df["Monthly_Spending"],
                           df["Preferred_Platform"])

age_platform.plot(kind="bar", figsize=(10, 5),
                  width=0.7)
plt.title("Monthly_Spending vs Platform")
plt.xlabel("Monthly_Spending")
plt.ylabel("Count")
plt.xticks(rotation=0)
plt.legend(title="Platform")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

plt.figure(figsize=(12,8))

sns.heatmap(
    corr_matrix,
    annot=True,
    cmap='coolwarm',
    fmt='.2f',
    linewidths=0.5
)

plt.title("Correlation Heatmap")
plt.show()
In [219]:
# 4. SCATTER PLOT WITH REGRESSION LINE


if len(numeric_cols) >= 2:

    plt.figure(figsize=(8,6))

    sns.regplot(
        data=df,
        x=numeric_cols[0],
        y=numeric_cols[1],
        scatter_kws={"alpha":0.6}
    )

    plt.title("Scatter Plot with Regression Line")
In [220]:
# DISTRIBUTION ANALYSIS
for col in numeric_cols:

    plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))

    sns.histplot(
        df[col],
        kde=True,
        bins=10
    )

    plt.title(f"Distribution of {col}")

    plt.show()
In [221]:
#Group wise analysis

categorical_cols = df.select_dtypes(include='object').columns

if len(categorical_cols) > 0 and len(numeric_cols) > 0:

    group_mean = df.groupby(
        categorical_cols[0]
    )[numeric_cols].mean()

    print("Group-wise Mean Analysis")

    display(group_mean)

 #Replace with actual survey columns LIKERT SCALE ANALYSIS
likert_cols = [
    col for col in numeric_cols
    if df[col].between(1,5).all()
]

if len(likert_cols) > 1:

    print("Likert Scale Correlation")

    display(df[likert_cols].corr())

# 14. SUMMARY STATISTICS REPORT

summary = df.describe().T

summary["Variance"] = df.var(numeric_only=True)
summary["Median"] = df.median(numeric_only=True)

display(summary)

print("Analysis Completed Successfully")
